key_insight: Materials Spatial Intelligence (MSI) learns directly from multimodal spatial microstructure data to reveal which microstructural features govern material behavior, not just predict it.
engineering_relevance: The framework can improve alloy design by supporting interpretable property prediction, trade-off analysis, and feature-driven microstructure optimization.
method: Encodes high-resolution microstructural and deformation observations into shared latent representations.
Preserves spatial relationships across modalities rather than collapsing them into handcrafted descriptors.
Uses the learned representation for prediction, interpretation, and optimization of mechanical behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that MSI identifies key features governing mechanical behavior and property trade-offs in structural alloys using multimodal spatial observations.
watch_item: The approach likely depends on high-quality, high-resolution multimodal data, which may limit transfer to sparsely measured or noisy experimental settings.
paper_title: Interpretable Material Spatial Intelligence for Discovery of Governing Microstructural Features
